title: [CI:DOCS] Build and Sign Mac Pkginstaller
body:
Create a new GitHub Action that builds and signs the Mac pkginstaller. The action also uploads the installers to the release, and updates the shasums file.

This action is triggered on release creation, but it can also be triggered manually via a workflow dispatch.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @cevich Updated, PTanotherL, thanks for the thorough reviews!

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ashley-cui*, *cevich*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [ashley-cui,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@ashley-cui is there a way you can do a manual test-run of this after it merges?  It would be good to identify any problems before it's time to do a real release.
@cevich I can, it requires me to mess with some of the existing artifacts on a github release page though, I think the best one to mess with is probably 4.6.0-rc1?
Maybe a better choice would be to make a really obvious "testing_do_not_use" release?  Up to you, point is to make sure there are no surprises since this job runs so infrequently.
In fact...that gives me an idea.  Maybe we should have a "dry-run" mode for these workflows, where they do everything except actually uploading the files.  I can work on that.

I think it requires a tag so a testing release won't work correctly. I'm not opposed to just waiting for the next release to go out and seeing if it works or not, because worst case we just end up building the binaries manually. A dry-run mode sounds good to me, but it also requires removing the files already exists check.

Though normally not advised, it's possible to delete tags and releases.  So you could create a tag called, `v6.6.6-testing` (just a silly example) "release" to it, and delete it all after.  

I could be okay with waiting.  It makes me slightly nervous, because a knowledgable/caring person doing the release may not have github-action workflow help available to fix it.  But maybe it's okay that the MacOS/Windows binaries are uploaded later?
I'll just run this once on 4.6.0-RC1, if something goes wrong I'll just re-upload the existing binaries.
Update: Ran on RC1, looks like it did everything correctly!
Great! Thanks for checking.  Opened https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19418 (but I need to test it somehow).
